decision.summary: external_mappings continues to reference card_prints only; card_print_identity informs identity law but does not replace the external mapping anchor.

decision_criteria.existing_repo_assumptions: Repo workers and audits already treat external_mappings as the canonical link between external ids and card_prints.
decision_criteria.downstream_breakage_risk: Changing the foreign-key anchor would ripple through pricing, ingestion, and catalog flows.
decision_criteria.canonical_object_stability: Mappings represent which canonical card object an external row refers to, not the internal identity-authority row.
decision_criteria.migration_safety: Keeping mappings on card_prints allows the identity subsystem to be introduced without rewriting external reference semantics.

hard_rules: external_mappings.card_print_id remains the external reference anchor
identity rows may inform canonical identity but do not become the direct mapping target
changing the mapping anchor requires its own explicit contract
